what_it_does: Database initialization, schema migration, and write/read helpers for a SQLite-backed recollection store. It maintains the legacy exchanges and tool_calls tables, adds a recollection data model (unit_type, atoms, units, relation_type, links, fingerprints, verdicts, candidates), manages per-lens sqlite-vec ANN indexes, and provides registry-driven typed-unit and relation-link writers.
observations: Uses better-sqlite3 and loads the sqlite-vec extension, with WAL mode enabled.
The legacy exchanges table receives additive column migrations; vec_exchanges FLOAT[384] is kept but demoted from the live write path.
Live vector writes go to a per-lens vec_<space> table whose dim, element type, and metric come from getLens/lensVecSpec.
int8 vectors are quantized with round-to-nearest and bound via the vec_int8(?) SQL constructor to satisfy sqlite-vec 0.1.9.
Portable fingerprint rows mirror ANN rows and carry explicit space, model, dim, element_type, and metric columns.
unit_type and relation_type are SQLite registries; unknown kinds raise UnitTypeError or RelationTypeError.
Mechanical relation types are seeded as directed inverse pairs: produced_by/produces, precedes/succeeds, contains/is_contained_by, references/referenced_by.
Link ids are deterministic md5(from|edge_type|to), making repeated graph builds idempotent.
insertExchange wraps the exchanges row, per-axis vector row, fingerprint row, and tool_calls in a single transaction.
deleteExchange removes the live per-axis vector, fingerprint rows, any legacy vec_exchanges row, and the exchange row.
standouts: Per-lens vector index factory with a dtype-specific binding contract for int8 and float axes.
Registry-driven unit and relation typing with fail-loud errors for unknown kinds.
Additive migration strategy that preserves legacy tables while adding the recollection schema.
Two-part vector storage: ANN table for search plus portable fingerprint row for lineage.
Candidate staging table for gated unit types such as principles.
conventions: better-sqlite3 prepared statements and explicit transactions.
CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS, INSERT OR REPLACE, and INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotency.
Dynamic SQL identifiers are double-quoted and lens names are regex-restricted.
Explicit lineage columns and LineageError fail-loud checks.
Long inline comments encode design rationale, guide section references, and landmine codes.
concerns: Very long design-rationale comments may drift out of sync with the code over time.
Active axis table names are interpolated into SQL; while regex-filtered and double-quoted, dynamic DDL requires careful maintenance.
Legacy vec_exchanges remains in the schema even though insertExchange no longer writes to it.
round_id requirement for distilled units is enforced only in application code, not by a NOT NULL constraint.
TEXT appears as a deterministic symbol but is a SQLite type used inside DDL strings, not a TypeScript export.
notes: This file is the schema and write-gate layer for a hybrid legacy/recollection SQLite store, with strong opinions about fail-loud lineage, registry-driven types, and per-lens vector storage.
questions: Where are getLens and lensVecSpec defined, and what populates the active lens registry?
Which callers write atoms, verdicts, and semantic links, and how do they enforce round_id for distilled units?
How is legacy vec_exchanges data retired or migrated to the new per-axis fabric?
Is there a migration path to drop vec_exchanges once all rows are re-embedded?
How are candidate statuses advanced through staged, proposed, refined, committed, or rejected?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (schema migration + registry-driven recollection data model + per-lens vector storage) matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Portable-by-field absorption into a larger system
intention_signals: The file is trying to evolve a legacy episodic conversation store into a structured, typed, multi-axis recollection fabric that can be absorbed by a larger Company system without rewrites, while keeping the old store running.
novelty: The per-lens vec0 table factory with an int8 quantization and write contract, the two-part fingerprint/ANN row pattern, and registry-driven unit/relation types stored directly in SQLite.
contribution: A concrete SQLite schema and write API for typed units, relation links, multi-lens embeddings, and candidate staging, with deterministic ids, fail-loud lineage, and additive migrations.
summary_for_embedding: Database layer for a SQLite recollection store managing legacy exchanges, recollection registries, per-lens vector indexes, and provenance links.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a stable, additive, registry-driven persistence backend for a conversation recollection system migrating toward a multi-axis semantic fabric.

symbols.does: Adds missing columns to the legacy exchanges table and migrates dependent schemas.
symbols.description: Iterates over a list of additive column migrations for the exchanges table, runs any that are missing, then calls the tool_calls cascade migration and the recollection schema migration.

symbols.does: Error thrown when required lineage fields are missing.
symbols.description: Custom Error subclass used by assertAtomLineage and assertUnitLineage to reject writes that lack mandatory session, project, or round identifiers.

symbols.does: Descriptor for a per-lens sqlite-vec index.
symbols.description: Type carrying space name, vector dimension, element type, and distance metric used to materialize a vec_<space> virtual table.

symbols.does: Creates a per-lens sqlite-vec virtual table if it does not exist.
symbols.description: Validates the lens name and dimension, then executes CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE for vec_<space> with the specified dtype, dim, and metric, returning the double-quoted table identifier.

symbols.does: Resolves the currently configured lens into a table name and spec.
symbols.description: Reads the active lens via getLens, derives its LensSpec with lensVecSpec, and returns the lens, the double-quoted vec_<space> identifier, and the spec.

symbols.does: Ensures the active lens's vec table exists.
symbols.description: Thin wrapper around ensureLensIndex for the default lens; used during initDatabase so the live axis table is present before any writes.

symbols.does: Quantizes a float vector to Int8Array using round-to-nearest.
symbols.description: Converts each component with Math.round to recover intended integers from noisy float representations, used for int8 vector columns and fingerprint bytes.

symbols.does: Produces a SQL expression and Buffer for binding a vector to a vec0 column.
symbols.description: Checks vector length against the spec dim; returns vec_int8(?) with a rounded Int8Array buffer for int8 axes, or a raw Float32 buffer for float axes.

symbols.does: Creates the recollection data-model tables and seeds mechanical relation types.
symbols.description: Idempotently creates unit_type, atoms, units, relation_type, links, fingerprints, verdicts, candidates tables and indexes, then seeds the mechanical relation vocabulary.

symbols.does: SQLite TEXT column affinity used in schema strings.
symbols.description: Appears in CREATE TABLE DDL throughout the file to declare string-typed columns; not a TypeScript export.

symbols.does: Validates that an atom has required session and project lineage.
symbols.description: Throws LineageError if sessionId or project is missing, enforcing fail-loud lineage for raw atom records.

symbols.does: Validates unit lineage and round_id requirements.
symbols.description: Throws LineageError if sessionId or project is missing, and additionally requires roundId when the caller indicates the unit is distilled.

symbols.does: Error thrown for invalid or unknown unit types.
symbols.description: Custom Error subclass used when a unit references an unregistered unit_type or a candidate uses a non-gated kind.

symbols.does: Shape of a unit_type registry row.
symbols.description: Type describing name, scale, optional extraction prompt, embedding lenses, gated flag, and description for a registered unit kind.

symbols.does: Inserts a unit_type registry row idempotently.
symbols.description: Validates name and scale, then INSERT OR IGNOREs a row into unit_type so new kinds are added as data, not code enums.

symbols.does: Looks up a unit_type by name, failing loud if unknown.
symbols.description: Queries unit_type and throws UnitTypeError listing known kinds when the requested kind is not registered.

symbols.does: Returns all registered unit_types.
symbols.description: Queries and parses the unit_type table, returning rows sorted by name with embedLenses deserialized from JSON.

symbols.does: Writes or updates a typed unit in the units table.
symbols.description: Resolves the unit_type registry, asserts lineage, and INSERT OR REPLACEs a row with canonical source_address and lineage; does not write vectors.

symbols.does: Stages a gated record in the candidates table.
symbols.description: Resolves the unit_type, asserts it is gated, and INSERT OR REPLACEs a candidate row with status and basis; lineage is soft because it is pre-commit.

symbols.does: Error thrown for invalid or unknown relation types.
symbols.description: Custom Error subclass used when a link references an unregistered relation_type or a directed kind lacks an inverse.

symbols.does: Shape of a relation_type registry row.
symbols.description: Type describing id, directed flag, inverse, near/far spaces, label, and description for an edge kind.

symbols.does: Inserts a relation_type registry row idempotently.
symbols.description: Validates id and enforces the directed-with-inverse floor, then INSERT OR IGNOREs the edge kind into relation_type.

symbols.does: Looks up a relation_type by id, failing loud if unknown.
symbols.description: Queries relation_type and throws RelationTypeError listing known kinds when the requested edge kind is not registered.

symbols.does: Returns all registered relation_types.
symbols.description: Queries the relation_type table and returns rows sorted by id with directed converted to boolean.

symbols.does: Seeds the mechanical provenance edge vocabulary.
symbols.description: Registers directed inverse pairs produced_by/produces, precedes/succeeds, contains/is_contained_by, references/referenced_by as mechanical relation types.

symbols.does: Builds a file artefact node URI.
symbols.description: Returns file://<abs-path> for use as a stable graph node identifier in links.

symbols.does: Builds a session node URI.
symbols.description: Returns session://<sessionId> for use as a stable graph node identifier.

symbols.does: Builds a project node URI.
symbols.description: Returns project://<project> for use as a stable graph node identifier.

symbols.does: Inserts one directed crossing edge idempotently.
symbols.description: Resolves edge_type against the registry, computes a deterministic md5 id, and INSERT OR IGNOREs the link with direction/inverse stamped from the registry and mechanical confidence defaulting to 1.

symbols.does: Rebuilds tool_calls with ON DELETE CASCADE if needed.
symbols.description: Inspects the existing tool_calls schema, drops orphaned rows, and recreates the table with a cascading foreign key if the legacy schema lacks it.

symbols.does: Opens, configures, and initializes the SQLite database.
symbols.description: Ensures the database directory, opens better-sqlite3, loads sqlite-vec, enables WAL, creates exchanges/tool_calls/legacy vec_exchanges/active axis tables, runs migrations, and creates indexes.

symbols.does: Computes a canonical exchange provenance URI.
symbols.description: Returns exchange://<sessionId>/<lineStart>, falling back to the exchange id when sessionId is absent, for use as a stable source address.

symbols.does: Writes or updates an exchange plus its per-axis vector and fingerprint.
symbols.description: Binds the embedding for the active axis, then in a single transaction inserts/replaces the exchanges row, deletes/inserts the vec_<space> ANN row, writes the fingerprints row, and writes tool_calls.

symbols.does: Lists all exchange ids and archive paths.
symbols.description: Simple query returning id and archive_path for every exchange row.

symbols.does: Returns the most recent last_indexed timestamp for a file.
symbols.description: Queries MAX(last_indexed) across exchanges sharing the given archive_path.

symbols.does: Deletes an exchange and its associated vector and fingerprint rows.
symbols.description: In a transaction removes the live per-axis vector row, fingerprint rows, any legacy vec_exchanges row, and the exchange row, cascading tool_calls.
